metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the sibling-supersede detection mechanism: specify what data (e.g., a ticket field, a cross-reference in the attestation, a supersedes pointer on the child) makes a finding attributed to child A count as 'superseded' by a sibling under the same parent, so the executor can implement the predicate without guessing.
findings.finding: The 'superseded by a sibling under the same parent' branch of the `delivered-now` predicate is never defined: no mechanism, data source, or detection logic is given, deferring a real design choice to the executor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `delivered-now` definition: 'OR superseded by a sibling under the same parent'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC sub-bullet or a 'How sibling supersede works' note: e.g., 'A child C_A is considered superseded by sibling C_B iff C_B carries a valid completion attestation AND its plan explicitly lists C_A's AC id(s) as subsumed; detected by reading the sibling's attestation metadata.' Alternatively, defer sibling-supersede to a later epic and keep v1 to attestation-only delivery.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the calibration gold-set: either enumerate the case categories it must cover (e.g., security finding on a delivered child, contract finding spanning two open siblings, priority=CRITICAL finding on a force-closed child) or set a minimum count and reference a source (e.g., 'at least N cases drawn from the must-never-suppress taxonomy in the completion floor config').
findings.finding: The 'calibration gold-set of must-never-suppress cases' in the test AC is underspecified: the set's membership criteria and minimum size are not stated, leaving the executor to decide what constitutes a satisfying gold-set.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests: 'plus a calibration gold-set of must-never-suppress cases'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'plus a calibration gold-set of must-never-suppress cases' with an explicit list of 3–5 case categories not already covered by (a)–(c), for example: 'plus at least one case each for (d) a security finding on a delivered child, (e) a contract finding on a delivered child, (f) a CRITICAL-priority plan-semantics finding on a delivered child at or above the priority floor'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile 'evidence flag' (AC3) with 'config evidence-gate' (Scope/What we build) — pick one canonical term and use it consistently across all sections.
findings.finding: Terminology drift between 'evidence flag' (AC3) and 'config evidence-gate' (Scope/What we build) for the same gating concept.
findings.location: AC line 3 ('gated behind an evidence flag') vs Scope IN ('config evidence-gate') and What we build ('config evidence-gate + priority floor')
findings.suggested_fix: Use 'config evidence-gate' throughout (already the majority usage); update AC3 to read 'gated behind a config evidence-gate + configurable priority floor'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either link the decision comment location (e.g. issue URL, ADR number, or file path) directly in AC1, or convert it to a prose note outside the verifiable AC set so it doesn't masquerade as a checkable criterion.
findings.finding: AC1 is marked DONE and references only a 'decision comment' — there is no described deliverable or verifiable artifact in the plan body corresponding to it, making it an orphaned retrospective AC with no forward-looking coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC line 1: 'Brainstorm + decision recorded (a vs b vs hybrid) — DONE (decision comment; option (b)/hybrid).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a direct reference, e.g.: 'Brainstorm + decision recorded — DONE (see decision comment on #<issue> / ADR-XXXX; option (b)/hybrid selected).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the completion floor: name the evidence-flag, specify its default, describe what happens on flag-off (full re-surface on next run), and add a recovery procedure for the case where suppression is found to be incorrect.
findings.finding: The Pass-3 completion floor changes the output of REVIEW_RESULT for existing container-ticket reviews (findings that were present can now be dropped), but no explicit rollback/back-out path is specified for the evidence-flag or for a bad suppression event — the flag's default value, its off-behavior, and how to restore suppressed findings on flag flip are unspecified.
findings.location: AC line 3 / 'What we build' section / 'gated behind an evidence flag + configurable priority floor'
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC (or a new 'Rollback' section), add: (1) name the flag (e.g., `enable_completion_floor`), (2) default=false until calibration gold-set passes, (3) on flag-off → completion floor is skipped and all findings are emitted as normal on the next recompute, (4) sidecar retains the drop log so an audit trail exists even after flag-off.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a planned ADR (or a named doc artifact) covering the completion-floor pattern: its sub-call schema ({attribution, containment, layer} + fail-safe values), the `delivered-now` predicate invariants, the evidence-flag and priority-floor config contract, and the relationship to ADR 0008 (novelty floor).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a materially new architectural decision — a third Pass-3 floor (`completion floor`) with a new sub-call contract ({attribution, containment, layer}), a new `delivered-now` predicate, and a new configurable evidence-flag — but no new ADR is planned to document this pattern, its invariants, or its config contract.
findings.location: 'What we build' section / AC lines 2–3 / 'Scope' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the always-preserve criterion set (currently `{security, contract}`) configurable alongside the priority floor (e.g., a config key `completion_floor.always_preserve_criteria`), so new must-never-suppress criteria can be added without code changes.
findings.finding: The criterion exclusion set `{security, contract}` in the Pass-3 suppression predicate is stated as a literal enumeration in the AC with no mention of it being configurable or stored in a shared policy registry; if a new criterion (e.g., 'compliance' or 'privacy') needs to be added to the always-preserve set, it requires a code change rather than a config update.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC line 3: 'criterion not in {security,contract}'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `completion_floor.always_preserve_criteria` config list (defaulting to `["security", "contract"]`) parallel to the existing `completion_floor.priority_floor` and `completion_floor.evidence_flag` config keys; read it at runtime in the Pass-3 predicate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC (or Scope item) to author a new ADR documenting the completion floor architecture — the three-question classifier, the delivered-now predicate, the sidecar schema, and the exclusion from scope (deferred items) — parallel to ADR 0008 for the novelty floor.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new Pass-3 pipeline stage (completion floor) with its own suppression semantics, fail-safes, sidecar schema, and `delivered-now` predicate — a non-trivial architectural decision — but does not commit to capturing this in a dedicated ADR (only a 'decision comment' is mentioned). The novelty floor has ADR 0008; the completion floor has no equivalent formal record.
findings.location: What we build / Scope sections; AC line 1 ('Brainstorm + decision recorded — DONE (decision comment)')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the `delivered-now` / sibling-supersede predicate: require that the superseding sibling (the one with the `supersedes` link pointing TO the closed child) is ITSELF delivered-now (valid completion attestation), so a closed child replaced by an open sibling is not treated as delivered.
findings.finding: The 'in-epic sibling supersede' arm of the delivered-now predicate does not specify the direction of the supersedes link or require the superseding sibling itself to be delivered-now, creating a logic gap: a closed child B that was superseded BY an open sibling A would be classified as 'delivered-now' even though the replacement work (A) is not yet done.
findings.location: AC line 4 — `delivered-now` predicate definition
findings.suggested_fix: Define supersede-delivered as: a closed child X is superseded-delivered iff there exists a sibling Y under the same parent such that Y `supersedes` X AND Y itself satisfies `delivered-now` (valid completion attestation). Otherwise X stays in scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale explaining why a unified LLM sub-call (vs. a hybrid deterministic+LLM approach that short-circuits attribution for G3/G4 findings using the existing `_container_child` field) is the right mechanism for ATTRIBUTION across all finding types.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for choosing an LLM-scored three-sub-question Pass-2 sub-call for ATTRIBUTION over a lower-cost deterministic path that leverages the `_container_child` field already recorded on G3/G4 findings, leaving the design choice implicit rather than justified.
findings.location: ## What we build — Pass-2 completion sub-call design

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the sidecar schema extension for completion-floor drops: either a distinct `completion_floored_finding_ids` / `completion_floored_criteria` coverage key (parallel to the novelty floor's `floored_finding_ids`) or a `drop_floor` enum field on each suppressed finding record.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how completion-floor drops are distinguished from novelty-floor drops and normal drops in the existing `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar schema (`plan_review_result_v1`), risking schema collision that breaks offline observability joins.
findings.location: AC line 5 — 'Drops recorded to the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (joinable, observable)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `completion_floored_finding_ids` list and `completion_floored_criteria` set to the `coverage` dict (mirroring `floored_finding_ids` + `floored_criteria` from the novelty floor), so each floor's drops are independently observable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Surface the key alternatives considered in the brainstorm (options a, b, hybrid) and the rejection rationale for each directly in the plan body (not only in a comment), so the three-sub-question architecture's novelty is justified against simpler approaches.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a three-sub-question decomposition (ATTRIBUTION / CONTAINMENT / LAYER) as a novel atomic structure for Pass-2 classification, but the AC item marked 'DONE' merely records 'option (b)/hybrid' without surfacing what options (a), (b), or the hybrid were, or why simpler alternatives (e.g., a single composite predicate, re-running review only on open children, tag-based suppression) were rejected. Reviewers cannot verify the architectural choice is the simplest viable approach.
findings.location: AC item 1: 'Brainstorm + decision recorded (a vs b vs hybrid) — DONE (decision comment; option (b)/hybrid)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'Design Alternatives' subsection (3–5 lines) listing: (a) single composite predicate approach, (b) re-run only on open children, (c) the chosen hybrid, with one sentence each on why (a) and (b) were rejected (e.g., lack of fine-grained observability, inability to preserve cross-sibling findings).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and register the `plan_review_completion` structured-output contract (Pydantic model factory with `{attribution, containment, layer}` fields, full value vocabularies, and fail-safe defaults), create the corresponding `reviewers/plan_review_completion.md` prompt file, and register the contract in `passes.register_contracts()` mirroring the `plan_review_novelty` pattern.
findings.finding: The `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call's output contract is referenced throughout the plan but never defined: no structured-output schema, no registered Pydantic model, and no prompt file exist in the codebase for it. The analogous `plan_review_novelty` sub-call has all three (contract registered at `passes.py:147`, schema factory `novelty_model` in `verify.py`, prompt at `reviewers/plan_review_novelty.md`). The completion sub-call has none.
findings.location: AC line 2 ('A separate `plan_review_completion` Pass-2 sub-call…'); What we build section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `completion_model()` factory to `review_kernel/verify.py` (or `passes.py`) defining `attribution: str` (child ticket id or sentinel), `containment: Literal['limited', 'spans-open']`, `layer: Literal['plan-semantics', 'delivered-functionality']` with fail-safe defaults (`containment='spans-open'`, `layer='delivered-functionality'`), then register as `plan_review_completion` in `passes.register_contracts()`, and add a `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_completion.md` prompt file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a closed, typed vocabulary for each of the three sub-answer fields — ATTRIBUTION (e.g. the delivering child's ticket-id string or a sentinel like `'open-child'`/`'insufficient'`), CONTAINMENT (`'limited'|'spans-open'|'insufficient'`), LAYER (`'plan-semantics'|'delivered-functionality'|'insufficient'`) — as typed `Literal` fields with explicit fail-safe defaults co-located in the completion contract schema.
findings.finding: The three per-finding sub-answer enums emitted by the `plan_review_completion` sub-call — ATTRIBUTION (value shape?), CONTAINMENT ('limited' | 'spans-open' | ?), and LAYER ('plan-semantics' | 'delivered-functionality' | ?) — have their token names mentioned only as prose in the plan AC, never as a defined, closed vocabulary in any schema or decision-rule co-location. The fail-safe direction ('insufficient/n/a/spans-open/delivered-functionality all keep') is also only prose.
findings.location: AC line 2 ('{attribution, containment, layer} with fail-safe values (insufficient/n/a/spans-open/delivered-functionality all keep)')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the prose `{security,contract}` exclusion with a reference to actual criterion IDs from the registered rubric (e.g. criterion IDs that carry a `security` or `contract` tag), define these as a named constant or config list co-located with the Pass-3 drop predicate, and document the lookup rule.
findings.finding: The Pass-3 completion floor drop rule references a criterion exclusion set `{security, contract}` (AC line 3: 'criterion not in {security,contract}'), but neither 'security' nor 'contract' appears in the plan-review criterion registry as defined IDs, and no mapping from these labels to actual registered criterion IDs is provided anywhere in the plan or codebase.
findings.location: AC line 3 ('criterion not in {security,contract}')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the 'superseded by a sibling' detection mechanism: what event/field records supersession, how the predicate queries it (ticket graph traversal? a new event type?), and how it is distinguished from force-close/abandon — or explicitly defer this branch to a follow-on and remove it from the v1 `delivered-now` predicate.
findings.finding: The 'superseded by a sibling under the same parent' branch of the `delivered-now` predicate (AC line 4) has no detection mechanism, data source, or detection logic specified anywhere in the plan or codebase. The other branch (completion attestation via `_child_closure_findings`) reuses a confirmed-existing predicate. The supersede branch is a structurally undefined path that the Pass-3 floor depends on.
findings.location: AC line 4 ('OR superseded by a sibling under the same parent')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the completion floor's evidence-gate config key (e.g. `verify.completion_drop_active`) and priority-floor config key (e.g. `verify.completion_priority_floor`) in the plan, add them to `src/rebar/config.py` with safe defaults (`false`/`0.0`), and document them in `docs/config.md` mirroring the `novelty_drop_active` entry.
findings.finding: The evidence flag and configurable priority floor for the completion floor are unnamed in the plan (AC line 3: 'gated behind an evidence flag + configurable priority floor (mirroring the novelty floor)'), but the analogous novelty floor uses the concrete config key `verify.novelty_drop_active` and a named priority-floor constant. Without named config keys, the completion floor's gate cannot be wired to the existing `LLMConfig` or `config.py` schema.
findings.location: AC line 3 ('gated behind an evidence flag + configurable priority floor (mirroring the novelty floor)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add `completion_drop_active: bool = False` and `completion_priority_floor: float = 0.5` to `VerifyConfig` in `src/rebar/config.py`, document in `docs/config.md`, and reference these exact keys in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit 'depends on 457a, 6533, 94fd' ordering to child 77cf-4be0-a615-4757 and state that its end-to-end test requires all three implementations to be wired (not just stubbed).
findings.finding: Child 77cf-4be0-a615-4757 has no stated ordering dependency on children 457a, 6533, and 94fd, yet its end-to-end partially-complete-epic test and gold-set calibration cannot execute until all three are fully wired. Attempting 77cf before the others are done will leave the test suite with nothing to exercise end-to-end.
findings.location: child 77cf-4be0-a615-4757
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Dependencies' section to 77cf: 'Requires 457a (delivered-now predicate), 94fd (Pass-2 sub-call), and 6533 (Pass-3 floor) to be merged before this story's end-to-end test can run against the real pipeline. Unit-level gold-set fixture can be built independently.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and document the {attribution, containment, layer} struct interface between 94fd and 6533 (e.g., in a shared types module), and confirm 6533's unit tests mock that exact interface.
findings.finding: Child 6533-d3cc-7ffa-4d67 depends on 457a (delivered_now predicate) and 94fd (Pass-2 sub-call output) to implement and unit-test its truth table, but states no explicit ordering constraint or stubbing strategy for these dependencies. Its unit tests must mock both, yet the AC only says 'Unit tests cover the truth table (each single-condition flip preserves the finding)' with no mention of the interface contract with the two upstream children.
findings.location: child 6533-d3cc-7ffa-4d67
findings.suggested_fix: Extract the {attribution, containment, layer} output type into a shared module (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/completion_types.py`) referenced by both 94fd (producer) and 6533 (consumer), so both children compile against the same contract regardless of delivery order.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State an explicit protocol in 94fd and 77cf for what happens if the LIVE calibration run (77cf) reveals prompt wording issues after 94fd is closed — either sequence 94fd strictly before 77cf with a revision gate, or define a permitted wording-revision window before the freeze takes effect.
findings.finding: Child 94fd-c930-9e69-497e mandates that prompt wording be frozen at ship time ('Frozen wording (not regenerated per run) per G-Eval guidance'), while child 77cf-4be0-a615-4757 requires a LIVE LLM calibration run against the gold set AFTER the prompt is written. If calibration reveals the frozen wording mislabels gold-set anchors (e.g., a 'must-never-suppress' finding is classified as 'plan-semantics'), there is no stated mechanism to revise 94fd's prompt without violating its 'frozen' constraint — the two children's requirements are in tension with no resolution path.
findings.location: child 94fd-c930-9e69-497e
findings.suggested_fix: Reframe the delivery sequence: 94fd ships a DRAFT prompt (not yet frozen); 77cf runs calibration against the draft; if kappa >= 0.8, 94fd's prompt is then frozen and the freeze is recorded in the calibration doc. This makes calibration a gate for freezing, not a post-freeze audit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit integration note to 6533 and 94fd stating that 94fd's `plan_review_completion` function in passes.py should be merged before 6533's `pass3_decide` is added (or that 6533 depends on 94fd's merged state).
findings.finding: Both child 6533-d3cc-7ffa-4d67 and child 94fd-c930-9e69-497e modify `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` (6533 adds `pass3_decide`; 94fd adds `plan_review_completion`), but neither child states which ships first or how the other merges cleanly. Simultaneous development on the same file without a stated integration order creates a structural merge conflict risk.
findings.location: child 6533-d3cc-7ffa-4d67
findings.suggested_fix: Sequence 94fd before 6533 in the sprint board and note the dependency explicitly. Alternatively, have both authors coordinate on a shared feature branch for passes.py to be merged as a single unit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test case to child 77cf-4be0-a615-4757 that runs two sequential review passes on the same epic — first with a child marked delivered-now (findings suppressed), then with that child's attestation revoked/child re-opened (findings must resurface) — to validate the auto-resurface guarantee.
findings.finding: The parent's AC5 states 'a reopened/un-delivered child auto-resurfaces its findings' as a key correctness guarantee of the recompute-every-run design. Child 6533 claims the implementation ('no persisted suppression') but no child — including 77cf — includes a test case for the dynamic un-delivery scenario (e.g., attestation revoked, child re-opened). 77cf's end-to-end test only exercises a static snapshot (delivered + open + force-closed) and does not test that a previously-suppressed finding re-appears after a child's delivered-now status is revoked.
findings.location: child 77cf-4be0-a615-4757
findings.suggested_fix: In 77cf's test module, add a parameterised test `test_auto_resurface_on_undelivery` that: (1) builds the epic with child A attested → asserts findings on A are suppressed; (2) revokes A's attestation (or marks it drift-stale) → re-runs the review → asserts those findings reappear.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
